diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fat malabsorption from villous atrophy produces classic steatorrhoea.
Also causes steatorrhoea; distinguished by other features and testing.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unintentional weight loss (~5 kg) despite normal intake

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A cardinal GI alarm feature mandating malignancy exclusion.
Malabsorption causes weight loss despite adequate caloric intake.

symptoms.symptom_name: Refractory iron-deficiency anaemia (no response to oral iron)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Iron malabsorption from proximal small-bowel villous atrophy typically fails to respond to oral iron.
Refractory IDA requires endoscopic exclusion of a GI bleeding source or tumour.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intensely itchy grouped blisters on extensor surfaces of elbows and knees (dermatitis herpetiformis)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dermatitis herpetiformis is the highly specific cutaneous manifestation of gluten sensitivity — near-diagnostic when present with malabsorption.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bloating, excessive flatulence and crampy abdominal discomfort

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Malabsorption and bacterial fermentation of unabsorbed substrate cause bloating and flatulence.
Bloating/cramping is non-specific; the alarm features here argue strongly against a purely functional label.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute high-volume watery diarrhoea for 48 hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: High-volume losses over 48 hours drive the acute volume depletion.
Acute diarrhoea alone is non-specific; the chronic backdrop makes a simple infection unlikely to be the whole story.

symptoms.symptom_name: Syncope on standing / severe orthostatic lightheadedness (patient-reported)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A patient-observable marker of significant volume depletion / near-shock — the key driver of urgent escalation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unable to maintain oral fluid intake

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Failing oral intake against ongoing losses means oral rehydration is likely to be insufficient — supports the need for IV assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Orthostatic hypotension on measurement (BP supine ~95/60 to standing ~80/50, HR ~110)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective postural drop and tachycardia — a sealed clinician measurement; the AI Doctor infers volume depletion from the reported syncope, not from a measured BP.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dry mucous membranes, pallor and cachectic appearance on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinical dehydration signs — clinician exam; pallor is partly video-observable but mucous-membrane dryness and turgor require in-person assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Distended, tympanic abdomen with hyperactive bowel sounds and mild generalised tenderness without peritonism

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-specific malabsorptive distension; the absence of peritonism helps exclude an acute surgical abdomen but requires an in-person exam the AI cannot perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: Positive anti-tissue-transglutaminase IgA with normal total IgA

investigation_details.expected_result: Elevated tTG-IgA on a gluten-containing diet, total IgA normal (excludes IgA deficiency)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Coeliac disease remains unconfirmed; a gluten-free diet started empirically would then make future testing unreliable.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Highly sensitive/specific first-line serology when performed on a gluten-containing diet.

symptoms.symptom_name: Duodenal biopsy showing villous atrophy (Marsh grade)

investigation_details.expected_result: Villous atrophy, crypt hyperplasia, intraepithelial lymphocytosis
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Coeliac disease not definitively confirmed; also the opportunity to exclude malignancy endoscopically is missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Villous atrophy on biopsy (on gluten) with supportive serology is the diagnostic gold standard.

symptoms.symptom_name: No visible blood or melaena in the stool

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of frank rectal bleeding makes IBD less likely, though does not exclude it.
Absence of fever slightly lowers the probability of an acute infective colitis but does not exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: No chronic severe epigastric pain radiating to the back

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of the classic chronic pancreatitis pain pattern argues against a pancreatic cause of the steatorrhoea.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Steatorrhoea + refractory iron-deficiency anaemia + dermatitis herpetiformis + weight loss together give a very high pre-test probability of coeliac disease, but still require serology + biopsy to confirm.
High-volume acute diarrhoea + syncope on standing + inability to maintain intake is the time-critical picture that determines triage regardless of the underlying diagnosis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Anti-tissue-transglutaminase IgA plus total IgA (on a gluten-containing diet)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line coeliac serology; MUST be drawn while the patient is still eating gluten or the result is unreliable.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count and iron studies
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantify the anaemia and iron deficiency and inform whether parenteral iron or transfusion is needed.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum electrolytes / U&E (renal function, sodium, potassium)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess dehydration severity and electrolyte derangement driving the syncope; needed before/with IV rehydration.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Gastroenterology referral for upper endoscopy with duodenal biopsy
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms coeliac disease (villous atrophy on gluten) and excludes a malignant/inflammatory GI source of the alarm features.
Starting a gluten-free diet before serology and biopsy normalises the mucosa and antibodies and causes false-negative results — must be avoided.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Further escalation of oral iron alone
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The anaemia is refractory because of malabsorption, not inadequate intake; more oral iron will not help and delays proper diagnosis.
